description_long: This object is a vintage television with a boxy, wooden exterior and a prominent convex glass screen on the front. The casing is made of dark brown wood, giving it a retro aesthetic, and features a black, dome-shaped housing on top with two metal antenna rods extending outward. On the right side of the screen, there are three large, round control knobs arranged vertically, likely for adjusting volume, channel, and tuning. The back of the television is plain, with a small rectangular panel that may serve as a ventilation or access port. The overall design is bulky and solid, typical of mid-20th-century analog televisions. The object appears heavy and robust, with a hard, matte finish and minimal decorative elements.
description: A vintage, boxy wooden television with a convex glass screen, top-mounted antennas, and three large control knobs on the right side.
description_view_0: The left side view shows the wooden casing and the dome-shaped antenna base on top, with the three control knobs partially visible.
description_view_1: The rear view displays the plain wooden back with a small rectangular panel and the two metal antennas extending from the dome.
description_view_2: The right side view highlights the wooden casing and the dome antenna base, with the control knobs barely visible on the far side.
description_view_3: The front view features the convex glass screen, three large round control knobs on the right, and the top-mounted antennas.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, brown wooden bezel with a matte finish and no visible decorative features
attribute_control_button_layout: three large round knobs arranged vertically on the front right side
